summary: defi-threat-scanner exfiltrates host data and self-propagates via npm publish
details: Package ships multiple offensive modules (scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js, worm-injector.js) and a package.json lifecycle hook that perform host reconnaissance and exfiltration to a hardcoded GitHub Pages C2 (https://ddjidd564.github.io). On install, code in package.json line 32 spawns child_process, gathers hostname/user identity/environment, and POSTs the data to the attacker endpoint. scanner.js executes system commands (whoami, os.hostname, execSync) and HTTPS POSTs results to the same C2; it also enumerates the filesystem (fs.existsSync) for credential and wallet artifacts. wallet-scanner.js performs comprehensive POST-based exfiltration with multiple curl/HTTPS sinks and similar host-recon primitives. worm-injector.js contains a self-propagation routine invoking execSync('npm publish...'), the structural pattern used to push malicious versions into other maintainer-owned packages. The combination of hardcoded attacker C2, install-time reconnaissance, browser/wallet/filesystem credential search, and an npm-publish worm leaves no benign interpretation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
